MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-02-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JOHN KAMAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, SIXTH, EIGHTH, NINTH AND TENTH CAUSES OF ACTION, SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD, FOURTH AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION, AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION. NONE OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS IS A DISGUISED MALPRACTICE CLAIM OR ASSERTS THE SAME PRIMARY RIGHT AS A MALPRACTICE CLAIM. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS FOR FRAUD ARE NOT ALLEGED WITH THE REQUIRED PARTICULARITY AND PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO CORRECT THAT DEFICIENCY IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS FAILS SINCE THE ASSERTED MISCONDUCT IS NOT EXTREME OR OUTRAGEOUS AS A MATTER OF LAW. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JOHN KAMAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled as to the first, second, sixth, eighth, ninth and tenth causes of action, sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the third, fourth and fifth causes of action, and sustained without leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action. None of plaintiff's claims is a disguised malpractice claim or asserts the same primary right as a malpractice claim. Plaintiff's claims for fraud are not alleged with the required particularity and plaintiff is granted leave to correct that deficiency if he can do so in good faith. Plaintiff's claim for intentional infliction of emotional distress fails since the asserted misconduct is not extreme or outrageous as a matter of law. Prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
